desc: Oia is Santorini's signature cliffside village, known for caldera views, whitewashed lanes, and sunset-focused luxury stays. Peak demand is concentrated from late spring through early autumn, with July-August carrying the strongest crowd and pricing pressure, while April-May and late September-October usually offer the best value-to-experience balance.
hls: Famous sunset terraces and photo spots
Boutique cave hotels and high-end dining scene
Easy links to Fira and island-wide excursions
Long shoulder season with warm sea conditions

todo.name: Oia cliffside walk at golden hour
todo.desc: Explore the upper lanes and terraces before sunset for classic caldera panoramas.

todo.name: Oia-Fira caldera trail segment
todo.desc: Walk all or part of the dramatic cliff route for island-wide volcanic views.

todo.name: Ammoudi Bay waterfront stop
todo.desc: Descend to the small harbor for seafood and sea-level views beneath Oia's cliffs.

todo.name: Volcano and hot-springs boat trip
todo.desc: Take a caldera cruise to volcanic islets and swimming stops near thermal waters.

todo.name: Santorini winery tasting
todo.desc: Try assyrtiko-focused tastings and volcanic-soil wine styles around the island.

todo.name: Sunset catamaran cruise
todo.desc: Book a late-day sailing route for shoreline views away from village crowd chokepoints.

wifi.note: Reliable in most hotels and cafes, though evening congestion can affect peak-hour consistency.

prac.fltNote: No regular nonstop Gatwick-Santorini service all year; most UK access is seasonal nonstop to Santorini (JTR) or one-stop via Athens. Typical return fares are around £90-£190 off-peak shoulder dates, £190-£320 in core season, and £320+ in peak summer weeks.
prac.visa: Greece is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: July-August evenings can be heavily congested in Oia's narrow core streets and viewpoints.
Accommodation pricing rises sharply for caldera-view properties in peak season.
Cruise-ship schedules can create midday crowd surges in key photo corridors.

sweet: Late April-June and late September-October are usually the best months for warm weather, long daylight, and lower crowd pressure than peak midsummer.
